description: Canonical single source of truth for the Phase 3 transport contract: the immutable Phase 3 migration set (with locked sha256 checksums), the least-privilege worker role, and the function/table/schema privilege boundaries that the loaded migration chain must prove. Contains no UI commit SHA, no secret, and no production identifier.

invariants.sendIntentExactSnapshot: Every send intent is created at contract_version 2 / proof_version 2 (create_send_intent rejects any other value) with the locked draft subject/body/revision as sole authority, and is bound atomically at confirm time to an immutable draft_versions snapshot by a COMPOSITE identity foreign key (draft_version_id, workspace_id, draft_id, draft_revision) -> draft_versions (id, workspace_id, draft_id, source_revision), DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED, ON DELETE NO ACTION — proving the snapshot's exact workspace/draft/revision, not mere existence, while still permitting full workspace/draft graph deletion. Legacy proof_version-1 intents (NULL draft_version_id) are non-sendable fail-closed (transport.get_send_snapshot raises a uniform P0002). The worker reads confirmed content only through the private snapshot functions — draft_versions has no worker table grant.
invariants.mimeArtifactImmutability: transport.send_mime_artifacts rows are created ONLY via transport.create_or_verify_send_mime_artifact while the send attempt is in state 'claimed' (before smtp_in_progress); the worker has no direct INSERT. The function verifies sha256(raw_mime) = mime_sha256, exact size, the attempt/intent/workspace/message_id chain, and the 25 MiB bound; an existing artifact is returned only on exact identity (bytes/hash/size/message_id), never overwritten. Rows are immutable except the single retention-clearing transition (raw_mime -> NULL + cleared_at stamped, all metadata byte-identical).
invariants.mimeArtifactRetention: Raw MIME bytes may be cleared only after the attempt is completed/failed_before_delivery/cancelled; mime_sha256, size_bytes and message_id survive clearing as the durable proof; payloads are bounded by rawMimeMaxBytes (25 MiB) and never reachable by browser roles.
